title: sempervivum tectorum subsp. tectorum
intro: Sempervivum tectorum subsp. tectorum, commonly known as the houseleek or living stonecrop, is a perennial plant that belongs to the succulent family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The medicinal properties of Sempervivum tectorum subsp. tectorum have been attributed to its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and improve skin health, providing various health benefits including protection against infection and promotion of tissue repair. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a topical application or infused in tea to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
